summary: Iterative risk management lifecycle for high-risk AI systems (Article 9). Operators run this lifecycle continuously across design, development, deployment, and post-market phases; GovAI evidence packs may attach to individual lifecycle events.

lifecycle_stages.title: Risk identification
activities: Enumerate foreseeable harms for the intended purpose.
Catalog data, model, and operational hazards.

lifecycle_stages.title: Risk analysis and estimation
activities: Estimate severity and likelihood for each risk.
Identify affected groups and impacted rights.

lifecycle_stages.title: Risk treatment
activities: Select mitigations and record residual risk.
Link mitigations to engineering and process controls.
Trigger redesign when residual risk exceeds tolerance.

lifecycle_stages.title: Risk evaluation
activities: Compare treated risk against acceptance criteria.
Capture sign-off from designated risk owners.
Decide whether to deploy, restrict, or block release.

lifecycle_stages.title: Ongoing risk monitoring
activities: Track post-deployment incidents and metric drift.
Refresh risk register on substantial change.

risk_tier_taxonomy.criteria: Risks to fundamental rights, safety, or health when system used as intended or in reasonably foreseeable misuse.
Material operational risk that requires documented mitigations but does not block release.
Limited or transient risk addressed by routine engineering controls.

boundaries: GovAI does not assign risk tiers automatically; the taxonomy supports operator decisions.
Risk acceptance remains a human, accountable decision.
